source: Flocabulary G3 + Think SRSD Tier 2 G3

words.definition: wanting to know or learn about something
words.example: The curious girl asked many questions about the stars.

words.definition: to watch something carefully
words.example: We went outside to observe the birds in the trees.

words.definition: to travel to a new place to learn about it
words.example: The children love to explore the forest near their school.

words.definition: to find something for the first time
words.example: She was excited to discover a hidden path in the garden.

words.definition: to look at something very closely and carefully
words.example: The scientist used a magnifying glass to examine the tiny bug.

words.definition: to try to find out the truth about something
words.example: The detective wanted to investigate the mystery.

words.definition: to recognize or name what something is
words.example: Can you identify the different shapes in this picture?

words.definition: to ask a question to get information
words.example: She called the library to inquire about the book.

words.definition: to say what you think will happen in the future
words.example: Can you predict what will happen at the end of the story?

words.definition: to find where something is
words.example: Use the map to locate the treasure.

words.definition: not afraid to do something difficult or dangerous
words.example: The brave firefighter ran into the burning building.

words.definition: able to think quickly and find smart solutions
words.example: The clever fox found a way to open the gate.

words.definition: always supporting and staying with someone
words.example: A loyal friend will always be there for you.

words.definition: having the ability to do something well
words.example: She is capable of solving difficult math problems.

words.definition: someone you can trust and count on
words.example: My older brother is very dependable — he always keeps his promises.

words.definition: honest and able to be trusted
words.example: She is trustworthy, so I told her my secret.

words.definition: having no fear at all
words.example: The fearless climber reached the top of the mountain.

words.definition: being very careful to avoid danger or mistakes
words.example: Be cautious when you cross the busy street.

words.definition: very smart and able to learn quickly
words.example: The intelligent student finished the test first.

words.definition: able to be trusted to do what is right
words.example: She is responsible enough to take care of the class pet.

words.definition: the natural home of an animal or plant
words.example: The forest is the habitat of many birds and deer.

words.definition: the usual weather in a place over a long time
words.example: The climate in the desert is hot and dry.

words.definition: tiny drops of water in the air or on a surface
words.example: The moisture on the grass means it rained last night.

words.definition: an animal that hunts and eats other animals
words.example: The eagle is a predator that catches fish and rabbits.

words.definition: to move from one place to another, usually with the seasons
words.example: Many birds migrate south in the winter to find food.

words.definition: to stay alive through a difficult situation
words.example: Plants need water and sunlight to survive.

words.definition: to keep something safe and in good condition
words.example: We should preserve the forests for future generations.

words.definition: how hot or cold something is
words.example: The temperature dropped below zero last night.

words.definition: a long time with no rain
words.example: The drought made the river dry up completely.

words.definition: one of the seven large areas of land on Earth
words.example: Asia is the largest continent in the world.

words.definition: the answer to a problem
words.example: Working together, they found a solution to the puzzle.

words.definition: to work together with others
words.example: The students cooperated to finish the group project.

words.definition: to share information or ideas with others
words.example: We can communicate with friends by writing letters.

words.definition: to show how something works or is done
words.example: The teacher will demonstrate how to mix colors.

words.definition: to finish something or reach a decision
words.example: After reading all the clues, I concluded that it was the cat.

words.definition: to move closer to something, or a way of doing something
words.example: She took a careful approach to solve the problem.

words.definition: to put things in a certain order or plan something
words.example: Please arrange the books from smallest to biggest.

words.definition: something difficult that tests your abilities
words.example: Learning to ride a bike was a big challenge for me.

words.definition: to make something better
words.example: Practice every day to improve your reading skills.

words.definition: to try to do something
words.example: She attempted to climb the tall tree in the backyard.

words.definition: to make a picture in your mind
words.example: Close your eyes and imagine you are flying through the clouds.

words.definition: to think about something carefully before deciding
words.example: Consider all the choices before you pick one.

words.definition: to suddenly understand something you did not know before
words.example: I didn't realize it was already dark outside.

words.definition: to know the meaning of something
words.example: Do you understand how this math problem works?

words.definition: to remember something from the past
words.example: Can you recall what we learned yesterday?

words.definition: information and understanding that you have learned
words.example: Reading books is a great way to gain knowledge.

words.definition: facts or signs that show something is true
words.example: The footprints in the mud were evidence that someone walked here.

words.definition: an idea that explains why something happens
words.example: His theory was that the plant grew faster in sunlight.

words.definition: a series of steps to do or make something
words.example: Growing a plant from a seed is a slow process.

words.definition: a way of doing something in an organized manner
words.example: She used a new method to solve the puzzle faster.

words.definition: lucky; having good things happen to you
words.example: We were fortunate that the rain stopped before our picnic.

words.definition: to make someone very afraid
words.example: The loud thunder terrified the little puppy.

words.definition: a sudden feeling of excitement
words.example: Riding the roller coaster gave her a big thrill.

words.definition: great sadness or suffering
words.example: The cold rain brought misery to the lost hikers.

words.definition: easily affected by things around you; aware of feelings
words.example: She is sensitive to loud noises and covers her ears.

words.definition: calm and quiet, without fighting or worry
words.example: The park was peaceful in the early morning.

words.definition: hard to understand or explain; full of secrets
words.example: The mysterious box had no label or name on it.

words.definition: very interesting and exciting
words.example: The documentary about ocean life was fascinating.

words.definition: very surprising, in an amazing way
words.example: The magician performed an astonishing trick.

words.definition: to make someone unsure or unable to understand
words.example: The tricky question confused many students.

words.definition: to become bigger in number or amount
words.example: The number of students in our class will increase next year.

words.definition: to become smaller in number or amount
words.example: The temperature will decrease as winter comes.

words.definition: to put something new in place of something old
words.example: We need to replace the broken window.

words.definition: to carry or move something from one place to another
words.example: Trucks transport food from farms to stores.

words.definition: a trip from one place to another, especially a long one
words.example: The journey from our house to grandma's farm takes three hours.

words.definition: to walk slowly without a clear direction
words.example: The cat likes to wander around the neighborhood.

words.definition: to move slowly and gently, carried by wind or water
words.example: The boat began to drift down the river.

words.definition: to throw or move things in many different directions
words.example: The wind scattered the leaves across the yard.

words.definition: to become smaller
words.example: My shirt will shrink if I wash it in hot water.

words.definition: happening slowly, step by step
words.example: There was a gradual change in the weather from warm to cool.

words.definition: to take part in an activity with others
words.example: Everyone should participate in the class discussion.

words.definition: to give something to a group effort
words.example: Each student will contribute one idea to the project.

words.definition: to give an answer or reaction to something
words.example: Please respond to the question by raising your hand.

words.definition: to make someone believe or agree with you
words.example: She tried to convince her dad to get a puppy.

words.definition: to try to win against others
words.example: The two teams will compete in the final game.

words.definition: something people have done the same way for a long time
words.example: Making cookies at Christmas is a family tradition.

words.definition: the power to change how someone thinks or acts
words.example: Good teachers have a big influence on their students.

words.definition: a group of people with an organized way of living together
words.example: The ancient Egyptian civilization built the pyramids.

words.definition: a feeling of admiring someone for their good qualities
words.example: Show respect to your elders by listening when they speak.

words.definition: a person who offers to help without being paid
words.example: My mom is a volunteer at the animal shelter.

words.definition: to stay away from something
words.example: She walked around the puddle to avoid getting wet.

words.definition: to protect someone or something from attack
words.example: The mother bird will defend her nest from danger.

words.definition: to keep someone or something safe from harm
words.example: Wear a helmet to protect your head when riding a bike.

words.definition: to stop something from happening
words.example: Washing your hands can prevent the spread of germs.

words.definition: the chance that something bad might happen
words.example: There is a risk of falling if you climb too high.

words.definition: to depend on someone or something for help
words.example: You can rely on me to finish the work on time.

words.definition: a sign or action that sends a message
words.example: The green light is a signal that means go.

words.definition: the most that is allowed or possible
words.example: There is a speed limit of 30 miles per hour near schools.

words.definition: to help or hold something up
words.example: The tall pillars support the roof of the building.

words.definition: to divide into different parts or groups
words.example: Separate the white clothes from the colored ones.

words.definition: very, very old; from a long time ago
words.example: The ancient castle was built hundreds of years ago.

words.definition: easy to break or damage; needing gentle care
words.example: Be careful with that delicate glass vase.

words.definition: not found or seen very often
words.example: The blue butterfly is very rare in this area.

words.definition: very bright or very smart
words.example: The brilliant scientist made an important discovery.

words.definition: beautiful in a simple and graceful way
words.example: She wore an elegant dress to the party.

words.definition: very strong or violent; intense
words.example: The fierce wind knocked down several trees.

words.definition: moving very fast
words.example: The swift runner finished the race in first place.

words.definition: very rough, unpleasant, or unkind
words.example: The harsh winter made it hard to go outside.

words.definition: completely different from something
words.example: Hot is the opposite of cold.

words.definition: right or good for a particular purpose
words.example: This book is suitable for children your age.

words.definition: to do something, especially in front of others
words.example: The students will perform a play for their parents.

words.definition: a plan that lists when things will happen
words.example: Check the schedule to see when the bus arrives.

words.definition: to take something as your own
words.example: Our family decided to adopt a puppy from the shelter.

words.definition: to have something inside
words.example: This box contains all of my art supplies.

words.definition: to send something up into the air or start something new
words.example: They will launch the rocket into space tomorrow.

words.definition: to stop for a short time before continuing
words.example: She paused to take a drink of water before speaking again.

words.definition: to catch and hold something or someone
words.example: The photographer tried to capture the beautiful sunset.

words.definition: to bring things or people together in one place
words.example: Let's gather around the table for dinner.

words.definition: to arrange things in a neat and orderly way
words.example: Please organize your desk before you leave.

words.definition: to look at something again to study or check it
words.example: Let's review the spelling words before the test.

words.definition: to choose what to do after thinking
words.example: It's time to make a decision about which book to read.

words.definition: to stop working and rest for a short time
words.example: Let's take a break and drink some water.

words.definition: to watch and listen carefully
words.example: Please pay attention to the teacher during the lesson.

words.definition: to do something wrong
words.example: It's okay to make a mistake — that's how we learn.

words.definition: to do something one after another, not all at once
words.example: We should take turns using the computer.

words.definition: to try as hard as you can
words.example: Just do your best on the test and don't worry.

words.definition: to move forward or get better at something
words.example: She is making progress in reading every week.

words.definition: to not give up and continue working on something
words.example: Even when it's hard, keep trying until you succeed.

words.definition: to find the answer by thinking carefully
words.example: Can you figure out the answer to this riddle?

words.definition: to feel excited and happy about something that will happen
words.example: I look forward to our field trip next week.

words.definition: to think of an idea or plan
words.example: Can you come up with a name for our team?

words.definition: to show or tell someone about something
words.example: The teacher pointed out the mistake in my sentence.

words.definition: to have no more of something left
words.example: We ran out of milk, so we need to go to the store.

words.definition: to stop trying
words.example: Don't give up — you're almost finished!

words.definition: to have a friendly relationship with someone
words.example: She gets along with everyone in her class.

words.definition: to look after someone or something
words.example: It's your job to take care of the class hamster this week.

words.definition: finally; after everything else
words.example: In the end, the hero saved the village.

words.definition: used to show a different way of thinking about something
words.example: I want to play outside. On the other hand, it might rain.

words.definition: used to give one case that shows what you mean
words.example: There are many fruits I like — for example, apples and grapes.

words.definition: because of something that happened
words.example: She studied hard, and as a result, she got a great score.
